name: OLD SHAFT ELVET COLLIERY

surface_od_ft.qualifier: cc. (circa); value heavily overwritten/corrected, a subscript reads ~138 — reading uncertain

units: fathoms/feet/inches, transcribed verbatim — convert ONLY downstream

annotations: base of drift; depth equals thickness (unit starts at surface): 5fa 4ft 6in = 34ft 6in
seam name 'L. Main' = Low Main seam (Durham), named in original ink
marker horizon: base 14fa 5ft = 89ft; NOT contiguous with the drift above (seam top ~86ft 6in) — intervening strata are unrecorded in this summary section
seam glyph reads 'Hulton' but is the Durham Hutton seam; named in original ink
marker horizon: base 27fa 5ft 2in = 167ft 2in; NOT contiguous with Low Main above — intervening strata unrecorded
'167' written in ink below the depth column = total depth in feet (167ft 2in ~= 50.9 m), confirms the fathoms figure reads 27 (not 21)

seam_marks.how: named in original: L. Main (Low Main seam)
named in original: Hutton (glyph reads 'Hulton'; Durham Hutton seam)

notes: Printed BGS 'Section of' form used as a summary shaft section, NOT a continuous bed-by-bed log. Header: 'SECTION OF Old shaft near Elvet Colliery.' Communicated Sept 1947 by NCB (parenthetical illegible, possibly 'Brancepeth'). Date of boring/sinking blank (old shaft). One-inch Map 27; Six-inch Map 'Durham 27 NE/W'. Only three marker horizons are recorded (base of Drift, Low Main seam, Hutton seam), so the intervals are deliberately non-contiguous — do NOT expect previous depth + thickness to equal next depth; unrecorded strata lie between the seams. Surface level heavily overwritten (best reading 'cc.165' O.D. with a smaller subscript ~138, uncertain). Total depth '167' (feet) inked below the depth column; 27fa 5ft 2in = 167ft 2in = 50.95 m, matching the index depth 50.91 m. Page 2 is blank apart from a repeated printed table header ('Thickness / Depth from Surface: Fathoms feet ins') — no strata. Top-of-page marginalia ('79 42 46 / 46', 'NZ/24/SE') are filing/registration marks, not log content — ignored per rules.
